_meta.source_spec_note: No architect design artifact found in .hivemind/activity/planning/. Plan derived from: (1) orchestrator-mandate.md behavioral rules, (2) permission-denial-audit-2026-03-30 gap analysis, (3) current hooks architecture investigation.

goal: Implement mandate-enforcement hooks that intercept permission.ask and tool.execute.before to deny operations violating agent behavioral mandates, with agent-identity-aware denial messages.

baseline_evidence.tsc_clean: 2026-03-30T19:25:00Z — npx tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors
baseline_evidence.current_permission_ask: Auto-allows HiveMind managed tools + informational toast for writes. Never denies.
baseline_evidence.current_tool_execute_before: Records pre-execution trajectory event for managed tools. Never blocks.

step_1_authority_surfaces.schema: Mandate rule contracts — Zod schemas, agent profile types, check result types
step_1_authority_surfaces.shared: Mandate resolver — agent identity resolution, rule lookup, check logic, default rules
step_1_authority_surfaces.hook: Enforcement handlers — permission denial with mandate awareness, tool-level mandate blocking
step_1_authority_surfaces.plugin: Assembly wiring — replace inline permission.ask with extracted handler
step_1_authority_surfaces.test: Verification — unit tests for resolver, handler, and governance

step_2_concern_separation.defaults_slice: write — default mandate rules per agent role
step_2_concern_separation.handler_slice: write — hook handler extraction and enhancement

step_4_slice_sizing.splitting_heuristic: No splits needed — all clusters within ≤5 file limit

step_6_gate_definition.read_slices_return_evidence: N/A — no read-only slices in this plan

slices.description: Define Zod schemas for mandate enforcement contracts: MandateRule (forbidden tools/operations per agent), AgentMandateProfile (full agent role definition), MandateCheckResult (allow/deny with reason). Follow config-records.ts pattern.

delegation_packet.scope: Create src/schema-kernel/mandate-records.ts with Zod schemas for: (1) MandateRule z.object({ agent: z.string(), forbiddenTools: z.array(z.string()), forbiddenOperations: z.array(z.string()), denialMessage: z.string() }), (2) AgentMandateProfile z.object({ agent: z.string(), role: z.string(), allowedTools: z.array(z.string()), forbiddenTools: z.array(z.string()), customDenialMessages: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional() }), (3) MandateCheckResult z.object({ allowed: z.boolean(), reason: z.string().optional(), denialMessage: z.string().optional() }). Export from schema-kernel/index.ts. Follow config-records.ts pattern exactly. Include JSDoc with @param/@returns.
constraints: Use z from zod (already imported in schema-kernel)
Export all schemas and inferred types
Follow config-records.ts file structure
No business logic — pure type definitions

slices.description: Implement mandate resolver engine: (1) resolveAgentMandate(agentName) returns the AgentMandateProfile for a given agent, (2) checkToolMandate(agentName, toolName) returns MandateCheckResult, (3) checkOperationMandate(agentName, operationType, path?) returns MandateCheckResult. Define default mandate rules for all 10 agent roles from orchestrator-mandate.md.

delegation_packet.scope: Create mandate resolver in src/shared/. Two new files: mandate-resolver.ts (lookup + check functions) and mandate-defaults.ts (DEFAULT_AGENT_MANDATES map with rules for all 10 agents from orchestrator-mandate.md). Export from shared/index.ts.

hiveminder.denialMessage: Orchestrator mandate violation: orchestrators must delegate {operation} to specialist agents, not perform it directly.

hivexplorer.denialMessage: Hivexplorer is read-only. It must not modify code or state files directly.

hivemaker.denialMessage: Hivemaker implements — it must not plan, verify, or review. Delegate those to specialist agents.

hiveplanner.denialMessage: Hiveplanner plans — it must not implement. Delegate implementation to hivemaker.

hiveq.denialMessage: Hiveq verifies — it must not implement or plan. Delegate those to specialist agents.

architect.denialMessage: Architect designs — it must not implement. Delegate implementation to hivemaker.

hitea.denialMessage: Hitea tests — it must not write production code. Only test code.

hivehealer.denialMessage: Hivehealer fixes bugs — it must not plan or verify. Delegate those to specialist agents.

code-skeptic.denialMessage: Code-skeptic reviews — it must not implement or fix. Delegate those to specialist agents.

hiverd.denialMessage: Hiverd researches — it must not modify code. It produces research artifacts only.

constraints: Import MandateRule, AgentMandateProfile, MandateCheckResult from schema-kernel
resolveAgentMandate returns permissive profile for unknown agents (no false denials)
checkToolMandate and checkOperationMandate must be pure functions
No file I/O — resolver is in-memory only
Export from shared/index.ts barrel

slices.description: Extract and enhance permission.ask logic into dedicated handler. Replace inline permission.ask in opencode-plugin.ts with mandate-aware handler that: (1) auto-allows HiveMind managed tools, (2) resolves agent identity from hook input, (3) checks operation against agent mandate, (4) sets output.status='deny' with custom denial message on violation.

delegation_packet.scope: Create src/hooks/mandate-enforcement-handler.ts with handleMandatePermission(permissionInput, output, directory) function. This replaces the inline permission.ask logic in opencode-plugin.ts lines 154-171. The handler must: (1) check if metadata.tool is HiveMind managed → auto-allow, (2) extract agent identity from permissionInput or runtime snapshot, (3) call checkOperationMandate(agent, permissionInput.type), (4) if denied → set output.status='deny' with denialMessage, (5) if allowed → show governance toast for write ops (preserve existing toast behavior). Export from hooks/index.ts.
constraints: Import from shared/mandate-resolver, hooks/runtime-loader/tool-governance, hooks/soft-governance
Preserve existing auto-allow behavior for HiveMind managed tools
Preserve existing toast behavior for write operations
Add output.status='deny' path for mandate violations
Hook handler signature: async (permissionInput, output) => Promise<void>
CQRS: handler reads mandate rules, writes to output.status only

slices.description: Create mandate-aware tool governance that enhances tool.execute.before with mandate checking. For non-managed tools, resolve agent identity and check if the agent's mandate allows the tool. If not, indicate denial.

delegation_packet.scope: Create src/hooks/runtime-loader/mandate-governance.ts with checkMandateToolGovernance(toolInput, output, directory) function. This enhances the existing tool.execute.before hook with mandate checking. For HiveMind managed tools → skip (already governed). For other tools: (1) resolve agent identity from toolInput or runtime snapshot, (2) call checkToolMandate(agent, toolInput.tool), (3) if denied → set output to indicate denial with custom message. Export from runtime-loader/index.ts.
constraints: Import from shared/mandate-resolver, hooks/runtime-loader/tool-governance (isHivemindManagedTool)
Preserve existing recordToolEvent behavior for managed tools
Do NOT duplicate managed tool tracking — delegate to existing recordToolEvent
Add mandate check for non-managed tools only
Must not write files — CQRS read-only hook logic
Export from runtime-loader/index.ts barrel

slices.description: Wire mandate enforcement handlers into plugin assembly. Replace inline permission.ask logic (lines 154-171) with handleMandatePermission call. Enhance tool.execute.before (lines 172-177) with checkMandateToolGovernance call.

delegation_packet.scope: Modify src/plugin/opencode-plugin.ts to wire mandate enforcement: (1) Import handleMandatePermission from hooks/mandate-enforcement-handler, (2) Import checkMandateToolGovernance from hooks/runtime-loader/mandate-governance, (3) Replace inline permission.ask (lines 154-171) with call to handleMandatePermission(permissionInput, output, directory), (4) Enhance tool.execute.before (lines 172-177) to call checkMandateToolGovernance(toolInput, output, directory) BEFORE recordToolEvent. Preserve all existing imports and behavior.
constraints: Plugin is assembly-only — no business logic
Preserve ALL existing hook behavior (event, chat.message, shell.env, command.execute.before, etc.)
Only modify permission.ask and tool.execute.before sections
Import new handlers at top of file with other hook imports
Pass directory parameter to handlers (consistent with other handlers)

slices.description: Unit tests for mandate resolver: resolveAgentMandate returns correct profile for known agents, permissive profile for unknown agents, checkToolMandate blocks orchestrator from Read/Glob/Grep, allows hivexplorer to use Read/Glob/Grep, checkOperationMandate blocks hivexplorer from writing, allows hivemaker to write, denial messages are agent-specific.

delegation_packet.scope: Create tests/hooks/mandate-resolver.test.ts with comprehensive unit tests for the mandate resolver module. Test resolveAgentMandate, checkToolMandate, and checkOperationMandate for all 10 agent roles. Verify: (1) correct profiles for known agents, (2) permissive fallback for unknown agents, (3) tool blocking works for orchestrator (Read/Glob/Grep denied), (4) tool allowance works for hivexplorer (Read/Glob/Grep allowed), (5) operation blocking works for hivexplorer (write denied), (6) operation allowance works for hivemaker (write allowed), (7) denial messages are role-specific. Use Node.js built-in test runner.
constraints: Use import from shared/mandate-resolver and shared/mandate-defaults
Use Node.js built-in test runner (test, describe, it pattern)
No external test dependencies
Test all 10 agent roles from mandate-defaults
Test unknown agent (permissive fallback)

slices.description: Unit tests for enforcement handler and tool governance: handleMandatePermission auto-allows HiveMind managed tools, denies write for hivexplorer, allows write for hivemaker, checkMandateToolGovernance blocks orchestrator from Read tool, allows hivexplorer to use Read tool, denial messages contain role-specific text.

delegation_packet.scope: Create tests/hooks/mandate-enforcement-handler.test.ts with unit tests for handleMandatePermission and checkMandateToolGovernance. Mock the directory and runtime snapshot. Test: (1) managed tools auto-allowed, (2) hivexplorer denied on write, (3) hivemaker allowed on write, (4) orchestrator denied on Read/Glob/Grep tools, (5) hivexplorer allowed on Read/Glob/Grep, (6) custom denial messages per role. Use Node.js built-in test runner.
constraints: Use Node.js built-in test runner
Mock output object: { status: undefined }
Mock permissionInput: { type, metadata: { tool } }
Mock toolInput: { tool, sessionID }
Test both handler functions
Verify denial messages are role-specific

slices.description: INTEGRATION GATE — Run full verification after all slices complete. This is NOT a code slice — it is the final gate that verifies the entire implementation works together.

delegation_packet.scope: Run integration verification for mandate-enforcement hook implementation. Execute: (1) npx tsc --noEmit — must pass with zero errors, (2) npm test — all tests must pass, (3) npx tsx --test tests/hooks/mandate-resolver.test.ts — all resolver tests pass, (4) npx tsx --test tests/hooks/mandate-enforcement-handler.test.ts — all handler tests pass. Report PASS/FAIL with evidence for each check.
constraints: Evidence-based verification only — no code changes
Report actual command output, not summaries
If any check fails, report the specific failure with file/line
Do NOT modify any files

independence_proof.note: 04 modifies src/hooks/mandate-enforcement-handler.ts + src/hooks/index.ts; 05 modifies src/hooks/runtime-loader/mandate-governance.ts + src/hooks/runtime-loader/index.ts — no overlap
Depends on both Wave 2 handlers being complete
07 and 08 can run in parallel (no shared files), but 09 must wait for both

risks.risk: Agent identity not available in hook input
risks.mitigation: The SDK may not expose agent name in permission.ask or tool.execute.before inputs. Fallback: use runtime snapshot or session metadata. If unavailable, implement graceful degradation — enforcement skips when identity cannot be resolved (permissive default).
risks.contingency_slice: If agent identity is unavailable, add a slice to implement session-based identity resolution via runtime attachment snapshot.

risks.risk: output.status='deny' may not be supported by SDK for tool.execute.before
risks.mitigation: Verify SDK hook contract. If tool.execute.before cannot deny, shift all enforcement to permission.ask (which definitely supports deny). Tool-level blocking becomes permission-level blocking for file-accessing tools.
risks.contingency_slice: If tool.execute.before cannot deny, merge 05-tool-governance into 04-permission-handler and enforce at permission.ask level only.

risks.risk: Over-blocking — legitimate tool uses get denied
risks.mitigation: Default to permissive (unknown agents/operations allowed). Test all 10 agent roles. Include override mechanism in mandate defaults. Start with conservative rules — only block clear violations from orchestrator mandate.
risks.contingency_slice: If over-blocking detected, add a governance override flag to runtime attachment settings.

risks.risk: Existing tests break from hook signature changes
risks.mitigation: Slice 09-integration-gate runs full test suite. If tests break, the integration gate catches it before handoff. Plugin wiring (slice 06) preserves all existing behavior.
risks.contingency_slice: If tests break, add a fixup slice to update test mocks.

architect_decisions_needed.decision: Should mandate enforcement be strict (deny) or advisory (toast + allow)?
architect_decisions_needed.context: The orchestrator mandate says MUST NOT, but strict denial may frustrate power users. The current governance is advisory (toast only).
architect_decisions_needed.recommendation: Implement strict denial with per-agent override in runtime attachment settings. Default: strict for orchestrator, advisory for others.

architect_decisions_needed.decision: How to resolve agent identity in hook context?
architect_decisions_needed.context: permission.ask input has metadata.tool but no agent field. tool.execute.before has toolInput.tool but no agent. Need to determine which agent is making the request.
architect_decisions_needed.recommendation: Use runtime attachment snapshot (already loaded in other hooks) to get current agent identity from session context.

carry_forward: Type-check baseline confirmed clean — all gates start from green
11/17 hooks implemented — mandate enforcement adds to existing permission.ask and tool.execute.before
No existing denial logic anywhere — first implementation of output.status='deny'
Agent identity resolution is the critical unknown — must verify SDK hook inputs before Wave 2
CQRS boundary: enforcement handlers must be read-only (no file writes from hooks)

feasibility.evidence: All target files verified: src/plugin/opencode-plugin.ts (257 lines), src/schema-kernel/index.ts (22 lines), src/shared/index.ts (exists), src/hooks/index.ts (14 lines), src/hooks/runtime-loader/index.ts (6 lines)

constraints.resource_limits: 8 slices across 5 waves — manageable context for single orchestrator session
constraints.timeline: Critical path length 5 — minimum 5 sequential delegations
constraints.dependencies: All internal dependencies mapped. External dependency: SDK hook input contract must support output.status modification.

ambiguity_residual: Agent identity resolution mechanism — need to verify SDK provides agent name in hook context
tool.execute.before deny support — need to verify SDK allows output.status='deny' for this hook type
